note:
这张地图将从选举日开始使用实时结果进行更新，因此TikTok用户可以随时从应用程序中查看它，以获取最新的信息。
除了美联社，扩大后的选举指南将包括全国国务卿协会关于投票过程本身的常见问题。这一部分帮助解释TikTok年轻用户群可能不熟悉的细节--他们中的许多人都是在这次选举中第一次投票。这些信息汇总在APP中，包括选举结果是如何编制的，以及在计票过程中会发生什么。
TikTok还将链接到美国选举援助委员会(EAC)网站，以获得有关投票地点和时间的信息。
它还将链接到选举保护热线号码，该号码为投票困难提供帮助(英语、西班牙语、亚洲语言和阿拉伯语，以及美国手语的视频通话选项)。
选举指南上个月首次在TikTok应用程序中推出，目的是帮助TikTok的1亿美国用户与合作组织联系起来，这些组织提供候选人信息、如何投票、媒体素养等。在发布时，该指南包括来自全国国务卿协会、BallotReady、SignVote和其他几个人的信息。
这些信息是以一种易于阅读的格式组织起来的，但TikTok本身并没有创建内容-它是从合作伙伴那里提取出来的，并相应地被引用。在其他情况下，TikTok提供了一个简短的信息片段，并提供了一个指向合作伙伴网站的链接，以“了解更多信息”。
今天，TikTok用户可以通过横幅的方式指向该指南，横幅出现在所有与选举相关的视频中。他们也可以选择直接从TikTok的发现页面访问它，在选举季节，它在那里有一个永久的家。
有了更新，如果用户遇到关于选举的视频-例如，如果视频讨论了当前的结果-用户可以点击链接来查看美联社的实时选举地图。这可能很有用，因为TikTok的反馈视频并不总是最新的。
TikTok几个月来一直在积累与选举相关的资源。今年夏天，它宣布扩大与PolitiFact的合作伙伴关系，并在其APP中领导故事，以事实核查与2020年美国大选相关的错误信息，此外，他们还帮助处理与新冠肺炎、气候变化等主题相关的错误信息。
它还声称正在与包括美国国土安全部在内的专家合作，以保护其平台免受外国影响。它还禁止深水假货。
与其他社交媒体平台一样，TikTok表示不接受政治广告。然而，与Facebook和谷歌不同的是，这两家公司只承诺在选举日前后暂停这些广告，TikTok在2019年10月宣布了这一决定，解释说政治广告的性质与其用户在其平台上期望的体验不符。
在今天关于扩大选举指南的新闻之前，TikTok还启动了一个选举安全中心，以提高其政策如何应用于一系列与选举相关的内容的透明度，包括内容何时以及为什么被删除。
虽然大型科技平台向其用户群提供与选举相关的资源是很常见的-Facebook、谷歌、Snapchat和Twitter都是这样做的-但TikTok的地位是独一无二的。这款应用程序目前正试图击退特朗普政府对其应用程序在美国的禁令，其在美国的长期命运仍不得而知。
尽管存在这些问题，TikTok在开发新功能、增加资源或以其他方式扩展平台方面并没有放慢脚步。例如，就在昨天，TikTok宣布与Shopify就社交商务计划建立合作伙伴关系。
TikTok的新选举指南资源将于11月3日在美国推出。
note_en:
This map will be updated with live results starting on Election Day, so TikTok users can check it at any time from within the app to get the most current information.
 In addition to the AP, the expanded election guide will include FAQs from the National Association of Secretaries of State about the voting process itself. This section helps to explain details that may be new to TikTok’s younger user base — many of whom voted for the first time in this election. This information, which is summarized in the app, includes how election results are compiled and what to expect during the counting process.
   TikTok will also link out to the U.S. Election Assistance Commission (EAC) website for information about polling locations and hours.
  And it will link to the Election Protection Hotline number, which provides assistance with voting difficulties (in English, Spanish, Asian languages and Arabic, as well as a video call option for American Sign Language).
  The election guide was  first introduced in the TikTok app last month to help connect TikTok’s 100 million U.S. users to partner organizations that offered information about the candidates, how to vote, media literacy and more. At launch, the guide included information from the  National Association of Secretaries of State,  BallotReady,  SignVote and several others.
 The information is organized in an easy-to-read format, but TikTok itself is not creating the content — it’s pulled from partners and cited accordingly. In other cases, TikTok offers a short snippet of information with a link to the partner site to “learn more.”
 TikTok users are today pointed to the guide by way of a banner that appears across all election-related videos. They also can choose to visit it directly from TikTok’s Discover Page, where it has a permanent home during election season.
 With the update, if users encounter videos about the elections — for example, if a video discusses the current results — the user could tap the link to see the AP’s live election map. This could be useful because TikTok’s in-feed videos aren’t always the most recent.
  TikTok has been building up its election-related resources for months.  This summer, it announced expanded partnerships with PolitiFact and Lead Stories to fact check misinformation related to the 2020 U.S. election in its app, in addition to their work helping with misinformation related to COVID-19, climate change and other topics.
 It also  claimed to be working with experts, including the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, to protect against foreign influence on its platform. And it  banned deepfakes.
 Like other social media platforms, TikTok said it isn’t accepting political ads. However, unlike  Facebook and  Google, which only committed to temporary pauses on these ads  before and  after election day, TikTok announced its decision in October 2019, explaining that  the nature of political ads didn’t fit with the experience its users expect on its platform.
 Ahead of today’s news about the expanded election guide, TikTok also launched an  Election Safety Center to increase transparency about how its policies apply to a range of election-related content, including when and why content is removed.
  While it’s common for a large tech platform to offer election-related resources to its user base —  Facebook,  Google,  Snapchat and  Twitter all do the same — TikTok’s position is unique. The app is  currently trying to fight off the Trump administration’s ban of its app in the U.S., and its long-term fate in the country is still unknown.
 Despite these issues, TikTok has not slowed on developing new features, adding resources, or expanding its platform in other ways. Just yesterday, for example,  TikTok announced a partnership with Shopify over social commerce initiatives.
 TikTok’s new election guide resources will roll out on November 3 in the U.S.
title: TikTok表示，它正在与美联社合作，在其选举指南中提供对互动地图的访问，该地图显示了美国联邦和州选举的实时结果
title_en: TikTok says it is working with AP to provide access to an interactive map showing live results for both US federal and state elections in its election guide

note:
根据昨天刚刚公开的一系列最新公布的FCC文件，苹果的iPhone12系列产品能够对外部配件进行无线充电。VentureBeat的Jeremy Horwitz最先发现的文件称，这款手机“支持内置感应充电发射器和接收器”。苹果还没有正式宣布任何这样的功能，可以用来为未来的AirPods或苹果传言已久的Tile竞争对手“AirTags”充电。
“除了可以用台式无线电能传输充电器(冰球)充电外，2020款iPhone型…。还支持360 kHz的WPT充电功能，对配件进行充电，“其中一份文件写道。它列出了一系列具有新功能的iPhone的FCC ID，其中包括iPhone12 Mini(BCG-E3539A)、iPhone12(BCG-E3542A)、iPhone12Pro(BCG-E3545A)和iPhone12Pro Max(BCG-E3548A)。如果我们正确阅读文档，最大反向无线充电速度为5W。
IPhone 12系列支持苹果新的MagSafe标准，该标准在手机内部使用磁铁来帮助正确对准兼容的配件，包括一个新的兼容QI的无线充电盘。然而，苹果还没有宣布对这款手机进行任何形式的反向无线充电，而且它们似乎无法为目前市场上的任何设备充电。
如果这份FCC文件表明，iPhone12可能具有隐藏的反向充电功能。也许是新AirPods上的MagSafe？Https://t.co/QhFQtOgoRB https://t.co/OAKzzb5U3B pic.twitter.com/pqAVcjLCyN。
-Mark Gurman(@markgurman)2020年10月28日。
听起来iPhone12的反向无线充电可能是有限的，至少一开始是这样。文件称，“目前iPhone手机唯一可以充电的配件是未来潜在的外部苹果配件”，目前“只有当手机连接到交流电源插座时，才会发生反向充电”。联邦通信委员会的测试似乎只使用了一个壁式充电器和USB-C电缆，大概是每部iPhone12捆绑的相同的USB-C to Lighting电缆。
听起来，这项技术的目的是让你减少床头柜上的充电杂乱程度，例如，对插在墙上的iPhone上的一对AirPods进行无线充电。然而，正如该文件继续建议的那样，这种情况在未来可能会改变。未来的设计和附件可能支持真正的便携使用条件，主机-客户对可以放在口袋或背包中。
目前还不清楚为什么苹果在宣布这款手机时没有正式公布这项功能，尽管这可能是因为与这一功能配合使用的配件还没有准备好发布。彭博社(Bloomberg)的马克·古尔曼(Mark Gurman)猜测，这可能会与该公司即将推出的AirPods改造相配合，本周早些时候报道了AirPods的存在。
反向无线充电功能似乎不需要太多的新硬件，至少iFixit在拆卸新手机时没有发现任何东西。
note_en:
Apple’s iPhone 12 lineup has the ability to wirelessly charge an external accessory, according to a series of newly  unveiled  FCC  filings that just went public yesterday. The documents first spotted by   VentureBeat’s Jeremy Horwitz say the phone “supports a built-in inductive charging transmitter and receiver.” Apple has not officially announced any such functionality that could be used to charge future AirPods or Apple’s long rumored Tile-competitor dubbed “ AirTags.”
 “In addition to being able to be charged by a desktop WPT [wireless power transfer] charger (puck), 2020 iPhone models … also support WPT charging function at 360 kHz to charge accessories,”  one of the documents reads. It lists a series of FCC IDs of iPhones with the new feature, which include the the iPhone 12 mini (BCG-E3539A), iPhone 12 (BCG-E3542A), iPhone 12 Pro (BCG-E3545A), and iPhone 12 Pro Max (BCG-E3548A). The maximum reverse wireless charging speed is  listed as 5W, if we’re reading the documents correctly.
 The iPhones 12 series supports Apple’s  new MagSafe standard, which uses magnets inside the phones to help properly align compatible accessories, including a new Qi-compatible wireless charging puck. However, Apple has not announced any form of reverse wireless charging for the phones, and they don’t appear capable of charging any devices currently on the market.
 If this FCC filing is any indication, the iPhone 12 may have a hidden reverse charging feature. Perhaps MagSafe on the new AirPods?  https://t.co/QhFQtOgoRB  https://t.co/OAKzzb5U3B  pic.twitter.com/pqAVcjLCyN
— Mark Gurman (@markgurman)  October 28, 2020
 It sounds like the iPhone 12’s reverse wireless charging may be limited, at least at first.  The documents say that “currently the only accessory that can be charged by iPhones is an external potential apple accessory in future” and that reverse charging currently “only occurs when the phone is connected to an AC power outlet.” The FCC’s testing appears to have been conducted using only a wall charger and USB-C cable, presumably the same USB-C to Lighting cable bundled with each iPhone 12.
 It sounds like the technology is meant to allow you to reduce the amount of charging clutter on a night stand by, for example, wirelessly charging a pair of AirPods magnetically attached to an iPhone that’s plugged into the wall. However, this may change in the future, as the document goes on to suggest. “Future designs and accessories may support true portable use condition, with the host-client pair able to be placed in a pocket or backpack.”
 It’s unclear why Apple did not officially reveal the functionality when it announced the phones themselves, though it could be because the accessory which works with the functionality isn’t yet ready to be released.  Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman speculates that it might work with the company’s upcoming AirPods revamp, whose existence was  reported earlier this week.
 The reverse wireless charging functionality doesn’t appear to require much in the way of new hardware, at least nothing that  iFixit spotted during its  teardown of the new phones.
title: 美国联邦通信委员会提交的文件似乎表明，iPhone12机型有一个未经宣布的反向无线充电功能，可能是为未来的AirPods或传言中的“AirTag”充电
title_en: FCC filing appears to suggest iPhone 12 models have an unannounced reverse wireless charging feature, possibly to charge future AirPods or the rumored “AirTags”

AMD今天将推出三款新的RADEON RX 6000系列显卡，它们将搭载NVIDIA最新的RTX 3000系列图形处理器。有Radeon RX 6800 XT(649美元)，它与RTX 3080竞争；Radeon RX 6800(579美元)，可以与RTX 2080 Ti或RTX 3070相媲美；最后是Radeon RX 6900 XT(999美元)，与Nvidia的巨型RTX 3090竞争。
这三款游戏都基于AMD的最新rDNA 2技术，该技术也可以在PS5和Xbox Series X中找到。这些最新的Radeon卡将支持下一代游戏的硬件加速光线跟踪，AMD承诺在4K和1440p PC游戏中与NVIDIA正面交锋。
新的649美元的RADEON RX 6800 XT是专门针对NVIDIA的RTX 3080的，承诺在4K和1440P游戏中的性能与NVIDIA的最新卡相当或超过NVIDIA的最新卡，价格比NVIDIA低50美元。它配备16 GB GDDR6内存、2015 Mhz基准时钟、225 Mhz Boost时钟、20.74万亿次浮点GPU性能，以及总共72个计算单元。
AMD已经分享了一些早期的基准，显示RADEON RX 6800 XT在4K的战场V，边界3，使命召唤：现代战争，力量地平线4，以及更多的地方击败了RTX 3080。它也与“古墓丽影”、“齿轮5”和“永恒末日”中的RTX 3080相匹配。如果这些结果在更大范围的游戏中都是相似的，那么RADEON RX 6800 XT看起来肯定会带来坚实的4K游戏性能。
AMD自己的基准也类似于1440便士，测试了同样的游戏。我们显然需要在RTX 3080上完全测试这张卡，但看起来AMD已经在一些游戏中设法赶上或击败了NVIDIA的最新GPU。AMD已经实现了这些性能改进，同时保持功耗低于NVIDIA的RTX 3080。RX 6800 XT的功率最高可达300瓦，而RTX 3080的功率最高可达320瓦。
接下来是售价579美元的RADEON RX6800，它的目标是NVIDIA的RTX2080Ti。根据AMD的说法，在该公司测试的4K和1440P的有限游戏数量上，它击败或赶上了NVIDIA的老卡。它配备60个计算单元、1815 Mhz基本时钟、2105 Mhz Boost时钟、16.17万亿次浮点GPU性能和16 GB GDDR6内存。
有趣的是，基本款6800的耗电量为250瓦，仅比NVIDIA自己的RTX 2080 Ti Founders Edition少10瓦。AMD没有与新的RTX 3070分享基准，但如果它也能赶上或超过NVIDIA最新的499美元的卡，那么它可能只是1440p的另一个很好的选择。
最后是售价999美元的RX6900XT，它与NVIDIA的RTX3090不相上下。虽然NVIDIA已经将RTX 3090定位为8K游戏，但其24 GB的大内存使其更有可能被创意行业用作Titan的替代品。AMD没有选择在同等内存上增加内存，RX 6900 XT保持了16 GB的GDDR6内存，并因此提供了一个有竞争力的价格。
AMD在这里将其计算单元总数提高到80个，但核心时钟保持在2015Mhz，提升了2250 Mhz。这一切都带来了23.04万亿次浮点的GPU性能。从AMD自己的基准来看，相对于RTX 3090的表现看起来好坏参半。虽然RX6900XT承诺在战场V、Forza Horizon 4和Gears 5中轻松击败RTX 3090，但它在其他游戏中输掉了或者只是与之匹敌。
所有三款新的RADEON RX 6000系列卡都使用传统的2.5插槽设计，具有常规的PCIe连接和三风扇系统，可为卡降温。AMD没有像NVIDIA那样做任何花哨的工作来缩小PCB，也没有推出新的12针电源连接器。
你会注意到，AMD在其所有三款新的RADEON卡中都选择了16 GB的GDDR6内存。这比RTX 3070(8 GB)和RTX 3080(10 GB)上提供的容量要多，但比RTX 3090上24 GB的容量要少。NVIDIA的RTX 3080和3090卡也使用速度更快的GDDR6X模块，但AMD选择避免使用更昂贵、更耗电的高速模块。
相反，该公司的目标是通过新的Infinity Cache设计来提高效率。无限缓存是AMD内存改进的最大部分。4K的游戏通常会受到现代GPU内存带宽的限制，AMD希望用更大、功能更强的高速缓存来满足其中一些需求，而不是使用更高速的GDDR6X。
AMD正在使用基于该公司Zen3L3 CPU高速缓存设计的高速128MB高速缓存。它针对图形使用进行了优化，并承诺提供两倍的带宽。这一切都意味着，这些最新的RADEON卡可以更高效地将数据馈送到图形流水线，并在不显著增加功耗的情况下提供性能改进。AMD的目标，在这里是两倍的性能比以前的RX5700XT卡，只有一个轻微的颠簸，以功率吸引。
因为这些卡都是rDNA2，这意味着它们支持微软的DirectX12旗舰版API，甚至一旦上市就支持DirectStorage。这包括硬件加速光线跟踪和可变速率着色。在2021年的某个时候在Windows中完全启用DirectStorage后，某些NVMe驱动器上的加载时间将进一步缩短。AMD还有一个“愤怒模式”，可以轻松稳定地对这些卡进行超频。我们将拭目以待，看看这些所谓的高效卡能获得多少额外的性能。
虽然AMD承诺在4K游戏和更多领域与NVIDIA正面交锋，但这场战斗中缺失的一大块是缺乏与NVIDIA的DLSS相当的产品。NVIDIA的人工智能驱动的超级采样技术已经为支持它的游戏带来了变革，只需切换游戏设置就可以带来出色的图像质量和更高的帧率。
AMD告诉我，它在测试中有一个新的超级采样功能，旨在提高光线跟踪期间的性能。该公司承诺其超级采样技术将是开放的和跨平台的，这意味着它可能会出现在Xbox Series X和PS5等下一代游戏机上。AMD正在与许多合作伙伴在这项技术上合作，并期待着行业的大力支持。不幸的是，这将不会准备好推出这三个新的RADEON RX 6000系列卡。
RADEON RX 6800 XT和RX 6800都将于11月18日开售，RADEON RX 6900 XT将于12月8日上市。虽然AMD还不能达到或超过英伟达RTX3070的价格，但RX6800XT与RTX3080相比节省的50美元可能是相当可观的。这将完全取决于AMD的性能承诺是否符合NVIDIA的最新卡，因为它们可以为那些希望在未来几个月升级GPU的人提供一个很好的选择。希望AMD能够为不可避免的需求提供足够的库存。
note_en:
AMD is introducing three new Radeon RX 6000 Series graphics cards today that will take on Nvidia’s latest RTX 3000 Series of GPUs. There’s the Radeon RX 6800 XT ($649), which goes up against the  RTX 3080; the Radeon RX 6800 ($579), which can be compared to the RTX 2080 Ti or  RTX 3070; and finally the Radeon RX 6900 XT ($999), which is pitted against Nvidia’s giant  RTX 3090.
 All three are based on AMD’s latest RDNA 2 technology that’s also found inside the  PS5 and Xbox Series X. These latest Radeon cards will support hardware-accelerated ray tracing for next-gen games, and AMD is promising to go head to head with Nvidia in 4K and 1440p PC gaming.
 The new $649 Radeon RX 6800 XT is aimed at Nvidia’s RTX 3080 specifically, promising performance in 4K and 1440p gaming that matches or exceeds Nvidia’s latest card for $50 less. It comes equipped with 16GB of GDDR6 memory, a 2015Mhz base clock, 2250Mhz boost clock, 20.74 teraflops of GPU performance, and 72 compute units overall.
  AMD has shared some early benchmarks that show the Radeon RX 6800 XT beating the RTX 3080 at 4K in  Battlefield V,  Borderlands 3,  Call of Duty: Modern Warfare,  Forza Horizon 4, and more. It also matches the RTX 3080 in  Shadow of the Tomb Raider,  Gears 5, and  Doom Eternal. If these results are similar across a wider range of games, then the Radeon RX 6800 XT certainly looks promising for solid 4K gaming performance.
 AMD’s own benchmarks are also similar at 1440p, with the same games tested. We’ll obviously need to test this card fully against the RTX 3080, but it looks like AMD has managed to match or beat Nvidia’s latest GPU in a number of games. AMD has achieved these performance improvements while keeping the power draw less than Nvidia’s RTX 3080. The RX 6800 XT draws up to 300 watts of power, while the RTX 3080 draws up to 320 watts.
  Next up is the $579 Radeon RX 6800, which is aiming for Nvidia’s RTX 2080 Ti. According to AMD, it beats or matches Nvidia’s older card in the limited number of games that the company has tested in both 4K and 1440p. It comes equipped with 60 compute units, a 1815Mhz base clock, 2105Mhz boost clock, 16.17 teraflops of GPU performance, and 16GB of GDDR6 memory.
 Interestingly, the base 6800 draws 250 watts of power, just 10 watts less than Nvidia’s own RTX 2080 Ti Founders Edition. AMD hasn’t shared benchmarks against the new RTX 3070, but if it can also match or exceed Nvidia’s latest $499 card, then it might just be another great option for 1440p.
 Finally, there’s the $999 RX 6900 XT, which goes head to head with Nvidia’s RTX 3090. While Nvidia has positioned the RTX 3090 for 8K gaming, its large 24GB of memory makes it more likely to be used by the creative industry as a Titan replacement. AMD hasn’t opted to bump the memory on its equivalent, keeping the RX 6900 XT at 16GB of GDDR6 memory and offering a competitive price as a result.
 AMD has bumped up its compute units to 80 in total here, but the core clock remains at 2015Mhz with a boost of 2250Mhz. This all results in 23.04 teraflops of GPU performance. The performance against the RTX 3090 looks a lot more mixed from AMD’s own benchmarks. While the RX 6900 XT promises to comfortably beat the RTX 3090 in  Battlefield V,  Forza Horizon 4, and  Gears 5, it loses out or just matches in other titles.
   All three new Radeon RX 6000 Series cards also use a traditional 2.5 slot design, with regular PCIe connections and a triple-fan system that cools the cards. AMD hasn’t done any fancy work to shrink its PCB like Nvidia has, nor is it introducing a new 12-pin power connector.
 You’ll notice that AMD has opted for 16GB of GDDR6 memory in all three of its new Radeon cards. That’s more than what’s available on the RTX 3070 (8GB) and RTX 3080 (10GB) but less than the 24GB found on the RTX 3090. Both Nvidia’s RTX 3080 and 3090 cards also use faster GDDR6X modules, but AMD opted to avoid the move to more expensive and more power-hungry high-speed modules.
 Instead, the company is aiming for efficiency improvements with a new Infinity Cache design. Infinity Cache is the biggest part of AMD’s memory improvements here. Gaming at 4K can often be limited by memory bandwidth in modern GPUs, and AMD is hoping to answer some of these demands with a bigger, more capable cache instead of higher-speed GDDR6X.
 AMD is using a high-speed 128MB cache that’s based on the company’s Zen 3 L3 CPU cache design. It’s optimized for graphics use and promises to deliver twice the bandwidth. What this all means is that these latest Radeon cards can more efficiently feed data to the graphics pipeline and provide performance improvements without significant increases to power draw. AMD’s goal here was to double the performance from its previous RX 5700 XT card with only a slight bump to power draw.
   Because these cards are all RDNA 2, that means they support Microsoft’s DirectX12 Ultimate APIs and even DirectStorage once it’s available. This includes hardware-accelerated ray tracing and variable rate shading. Once DirectStorage is fully enabled in Windows at some point in 2021, load times on certain NVMe drives will be reduced further. AMD also has a “rage mode” for easy and stable overclocking on these cards. It will be interesting to see how much extra performance can be squeezed out of these supposedly efficient cards.
 While AMD is promising to go head to head with Nvidia in 4K gaming and more, the one big missing piece of this battle is a lack of an equivalent to Nvidia’s DLSS. Nvidia’s AI-powered super sampling technology has been transformative for the games that support it, bringing great image quality and higher frame rates by simply toggling a game setting.
 AMD tells me it has a new super sampling feature in testing, which is designed to increase performance during ray tracing. The company is promising its super sampling technology will be open and cross-platform, which means it could come to next-gen consoles like the Xbox Series X and PS5. AMD is working with a number of partners on this technology, and it’s expecting strong industry support. Unfortunately, this won’t be ready for the launch of these three new Radeon RX 6000 Series cards.
 Both the Radeon RX 6800 XT and RX 6800 will go on sale on November 18th, with the Radeon RX 6900 XT hitting stores on December 8th. While AMD hasn’t been able to match or beat Nvidia’s pricing for the RTX 3070, the $50 saving on the RX 6800 XT vs. the RTX 3080 could be significant. It will all depend on whether AMD’s performance promises measure up to Nvidia’s latest cards, as they could offer a great alternative for people looking to upgrade their GPUs in the coming months. Hopefully AMD will be able to supply enough stock for the inevitable demand.
title: AMD推出新的GPU：RADEON 6900 XT售价999美元，比RTX 3090便宜500美元；RADEON 6800 XT售价649美元；RADEON 6800 XT售价12/8，比RTX 3080便宜50美元，等等
title_en: AMD unveils new GPUs: $999 Radeon 6900 XT, coming 12/8 for $500 less than RTX 3090, $649 Radeon 6800 XT, coming 12/8 for $50 less than RTX 3080, and more

Coinbase宣布，美国客户现在可以加入Coinbase Card的等待名单，Coinbase Card是一种借记卡，允许客户在接受Visa卡的任何地方消费加密货币。Coinbase声称这是“在全球范围内使用密码的最简单、最快捷的方式”。此前，该卡只在欧洲可用。
世界各地有许多借记卡可以让你消费加密货币(你很快也可以用Paypal做到这一点)。但许多平台要求你将资金加载到卡上才能消费。Coinbase卡省去了中间人，而是直接从Coinbase账户提取资金。其他卡仅限于一种或几种货币；据该公司称，Coinbase卡支持30多种货币(并在完成购买和自动取款机取款之前将所有卡转换为美元)。
还有一个可选的奖励计划(目前只对美国客户开放)。你可以选择用恒星流明赚4%的钱，或者用比特币赚1%的钱。虽然没有发行费，但Coinbase表示，“可能会收取加密货币转换费”(这些费用可能会很高)。
在Coinbase的应用程序中，你可以跟踪最近的交易并奖励收益。你可以在那里管理你的奖励货币，也可以管理你正在消费的货币。
如果这听起来像是你感兴趣的东西，你可以通过Coinbase应用程序加入等待名单。这将有点等待--Coinbase表示，首批客户将在“今年冬天”获得批准。一旦您获得批准，您就可以开始使用虚拟卡消费。你会在“两周内”收到你的实体卡。当然，您需要有一个经过验证的Coinbase帐户才能使用该卡。
更正10/28，美国东部时间下午1：29：本文声明Coinbase卡支持9种加密货币，正如Coinbase在发稿时提供的FAQ中所述。发布后，Coinbase联系我们告诉我们FAQ中的信息仅适用于欧盟客户-他们的卡为美国客户支持30多种加密货币。
note_en:
Coinbase has announced that US customers can now join the waitlist for its Coinbase Card, a debit Visa card that allows customers to spend cryptocurrency anywhere Visa cards are accepted. Coinbase  claims it’s “the easiest, quickest way to spend your crypto worldwide.” Previously, the card was only available in Europe.
 There are a number of debit cards around the world that allow you to spend cryptocurrency (you’ll soon be able to do so with  Paypal as well). But many platforms require you to load funds onto the card in order to spend them. The Coinbase Card eliminates the middleman, instead pulling funds directly from your Coinbase account. Other cards are limited to one or a few currencies; the Coinbase Card supports more than 30, according to the company (and converts everything to US dollars before completing purchases and ATM withdrawals).
   There’s an optional rewards program as well (only available to US customers for now). You can choose to earn 4 percent back in Stellar Lumen or 1 percent back in bitcoin. There’s no issuance fee, but Coinbase says that “cryptocurrency conversion fees may apply” (and those can be  steep).
 In Coinbase’s app, you’ll be able to keep track of recent transactions and rewards earnings. You can manage your rewards currency there, as well as the currency you’re spending with.
   If this sounds like something you’re interested in, you can join the waitlist via the Coinbase app. It’ll be a bit of a wait — Coinbase says the first customers will be approved “this winter.” As soon as you’re approved, you’ll be able to start spending with a virtual card. You’ll get your physical card in the mail “within two weeks.” You, of course, need to have a verified Coinbase account in order to use the card.
 Correction 10/28, 1:29 PM ET:  This article stated that the Coinbase Card supports nine cryptocurrencies, as was stated in Coinbase’s FAQ that was available at press time. After publication, Coinbase contacted us to tell us the information in that FAQ only applied to EU customers — their card supports more than 30 cryptocurrencies for US customers.
title: Coinbase在欧洲推出Coinbase Card后，将其Coinbase Card(一种用于加密货币的借记卡)带到了美国
title_en: Coinbase brings its Coinbase Card, a debit Visa card for cryptocurrency, to the US, after launching in Europe

在硅谷打击极右翼QAnon阴谋论数月后，数十家网站、社交媒体应用和电子商务平台仍充斥着其被揭穿的说法，显示出无论结果如何，其持久性都可能超过下周的总统选举。
QAnon在Parler和Telegram等社交媒体应用程序上的账户急剧增长，这些应用程序几乎没有内容审核。销售QAnon主题书籍和T恤的商家似乎正在蓬勃发展，包括亚马逊(Amazon)等大型在线零售商。最新研究显示，即使在寻求限制QAnon内容的科技公司之一Twitter上，公开兜售QAnon的账户在网络政治辩论中也依然活跃，特别是在佛罗里达州和德克萨斯州等关键州。
其结果是一场不断扩大的阴谋论运动，尽管硅谷努力清理其服务，但在竞争激烈的总统选举的最后几天，阴谋论运动仍然是虚假信息的主要来源。
QAnon账户最近尤其积极地散布有关民主党总统候选人乔·拜登(Joe Biden)儿子亨特·拜登(Hunter Biden)所谓的商业交易的未经证实的说法。这些账户还试图破坏人们对邮寄投票的信心，在没有证据的情况下警告说，选举将被民主党人“窃取”。
前FBI分析师、参议院调查员丹尼尔·J·琼斯(Daniel J.Jones)说，“尽管社交媒体平台采取了遏制QAnon增长的行动，QAnon阴谋论和错误信息仍在网上以惊人的速度增长。”琼斯现在是研究虚假信息的先进民主组织(Advance Democratic)的主席。“这种增长会产生现实世界的后果。我们将看到人们对我们机构的信心继续受到侵蚀，这将使治理变得越来越困难。“。
与此同时，兜售QAnon毫无根据的说法的网站已经迅速适应了将其击落线下的努力。
追踪网络极端主义的赛德情报集团(SITE Intelligence Group)的研究显示，在俄勒冈州的一家公司切断了与匿名留言板8kun的联系后，匿名留言板8kun本月开始使用一家俄罗斯公司来保护自己免受网络攻击。神秘人物“Q”就是在这里发布被称为“Drops”的神秘线索的。
它还发现，其他QAnon网站也得到了类似的保护，来自旧金山的Cloudflare。CloudFlare的公共政策负责人Alissa Starzak在一份声明中说，“未经用户许可，我们不会讨论他们。”
2017年10月，QAnon在匿名留言板4chan上诞生后不久，它就传播到了Facebook、Twitter、Reddit和YouTube等主流平台，这推动了更多的受众对民主党运行的撒旦恋童癖者的虚假说法。这些平台最终进行了打击-2018年的Reddit，以及过去几个月的Facebook、Twitter和YouTube-因为大量违反了反对仇恨言论的政策，以及对其助长现实世界暴力倾向的越来越多的宣传。
但每次有公司对Qanon采取行动时，阴谋论者都会为他们的信息找到新的场所，同时也学会在旧的场所上伪装-使用编码语言或调整标签，以避免自动关闭账户和删除内容。
这种适应性为Qanon辉煌的一年画上了句号。今年春天，Qanon通过散布有关冠状病毒起源和性质的错误信息，将其描述为微软(Microsoft)联合创始人比尔·盖茨(Bill Gates)策划的骗局和“崇拜撒旦的阴谋集团”，以迷惑和控制不知情的公众，从而实现了爆炸性增长。
关于疫苗安全性的虚假信息在网络阴谋论者中已经很突出，今年成为QAnon的一个关键叙事，同样的，在乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)被明尼阿波利斯警方杀害后出现的关于种族正义抗议的错误信息也是如此。SITE Intelligence Group 10月14日的一份报告显示，Facebook上的QAnon群组在该公司8月份最初努力控制后仍在大幅增长，抽样的85个账户显示，平均增加了100多名会员。
SITE执行董事丽塔·卡茨(Rita Katz)表示：“幸运的是，主流社交媒体公司现在正积极采取行动，让QAnon远离他们的平台。”“但要减轻QAnon的威胁，需要的不仅仅是知名公司。QAnon已经有了丰富的选择--如果不是整个基础设施的话--可以依靠。
根据Advance Democratic的研究，在摇摆州佛罗里达州和德克萨斯州，每25条关于选举的推文中，就有一条来自与QAnon有关联的账号，对特朗普推文的回复或其他参与中，至少有7%来自与QAnon有关联的账号。
这项依赖于数字情报公司Zignal Labs的数据的研究还发现，从1月到10月中旬，QAnon旗下的Twitter账户在推广从布莱巴特(Breitbart)到Gateway Popdit等极右翼网站的内容方面发挥了主导作用。与此同时，分析发现，佛罗里达州最活跃的政治账户帖子在本月早些时候被暂停之前，其名称和个人资料信息中都有QAnon的引用。
发言人劳伦·亚历山大在一封电子邮件中说：“我们致力于保护公众对话，促进推特上的健康对话。”到目前为止，通过我们降低QAnon相关内容和账户的工作，我们已经将QAnon相关推文上的印象减少了50%以上，这意味着我们的用户在他们的订阅源上看到的不健康内容减少了。“
Facebook发言人莎拉·波拉克(Sarah Pollake)表示，自从8月份扩大了针对煽动暴力的阴谋论的政策以来，该公司已经“删除了大约1700页，5600个群和大约18700个代表Qanon的Instagram账户。”
尽管QAnon的内容在主流服务上仍然很受欢迎，但移除的幽灵已经将追随者推向了更多不插手的平台。总部设在迪拜的加密通讯应用Telegram被赛德情报集团(SITE Intelligence Group)称为“国际QAnon社区的主体”，该公司尚未宣布对QAnon采取任何措施。该公司没有回复记者的置评请求。
QAnon也在Parler和Gab上蓬勃发展，Parler是一个在保守派中很受欢迎的社交媒体网站，它没有对阴谋论采取任何行动。Gab的创始人安德鲁·托尔巴(Andrew Torba)写道，他“欢迎QAnon”。帕勒没有回复记者的置评请求。加布没有对华盛顿邮报的询问做出回应。
随着QAnon信息在各种平台上的持续传播，与阴谋论相关的商业企业也在不断扩大--往往以对主流社交网络服务的执法行动为卖点。反虚假信息咨询公司Alethea Group的首席执行官兼创始人丽莎·卡普兰(Lisa Kaplan)表示，从T恤到咖啡再到马克杯，消费品就像模因和网络流行语一样，有力地束缚了阴谋论的追随者。
卡普兰说：“现在确实有一个与QAnon联系在一起的经济体。”“我们在选举的背景下谈论这个问题--首次有QAnon候选人可能当选国会议员--但我们对那些基于QAnon商品销售来支付抵押贷款和养家糊口的个人谈论得还不够.”
在QAnon阴谋论的发源地、庞大的在线网站8kun上，充斥着对所谓的深度国家的抨击，以及特朗普即将征服撒旦势力的隐晦线索，其中散布着更为乐观的信息。
“贴纸，”其中一条写道，“贴纸。”另一条建议是：“吃你的吧！”第三张同样是醒目的黄黑相间的，广告是“8kun咖啡”。
这些广告宣传了一家由吉姆·沃特金斯(Jim Watkins)运营的电子商务网站，沃特金斯是一名美国退伍军人和商人，在菲律宾经营着8kun。这个朴实无华、没有规则的论坛的居民认为这些广告是侵扰性的。“他们在跟踪我们，”一位网友最近在谈到这些帖子时写道。
追踪阴谋论的专家表示，这些广告也提醒人们，QAnon对沃特金斯以及其他提出欺骗哲学的运营平台的人和团体来说，是一个赚钱的机会。沃特金斯是与这些广告相关的电商域名的注册所有者，名为“还湿了吗”(Is It Wet Yet)。该网站与他在2月份创立的一个名为“解除深层国家武装”(Disarm The Deep State)的政治行动委员会有关，该委员会在Facebook和Twitter上都有自己的存在，并将自己描述为“第一个致力于清除影子政府行为者的超级政治行动委员会”。
“这显然是自我交易，”软件开发人员弗雷德里克·布伦南(Fredrick Brennan)说。他在2013年创立了8kun的前身8chan，但后来呼吁关闭该网站，并将批评矛头指向该网站的新所有者，该所有者最近导致沃特金斯对他提起“网络诽谤”诉讼。布伦南说：“他知道他完全控制了Q，不管他是否自己写帖子，所以他已经能够把它变成一个行业。”
布伦南说，这种“暴利行为”超出了沃特金斯的范畴，而且由于最近对社交媒体的打压而愈演愈烈。在各大科技公司采取执法行动后，阴谋论在很大程度上得到了一个强大的数字市场的支持，市场上的商品打上了它的品牌。
该理论的传道者批评大制药公司，然后向他们说服的人推广替代药物。他们对即将到来的社会崩溃发出警告，然后向新生的幸存者兜售“准备”设备。
QAnon市场架起了主流和边缘在线平台的桥梁。根据阿莱西亚集团(Alethea Group)和全球虚假信息指数(Global Disinformation Index)进行的一项分析，最受欢迎的网站是亚马逊(Amazon)，拥有超过8000个Qanon品牌的产品。
亚马逊图书至少有30种不同的QAnon相关文本，其中许多最终登上了宗教哲学、有争议的宗教知识和其他晦涩难懂的流派的畅销书排行榜。
布伦南说：“亚马逊是帮助QAnon有影响力的人赚钱的人，它甚至可能比Facebook更重要。”“他们需要支付流程，就像他们需要社交媒体一样。如果他们没有支付流程，他们的病毒式传播如何都无所谓--他们无法募集捐款。“。
亚马逊发言人玛丽·凯特·麦卡锡(Mary Kate McCarthy)拒绝置评。(亚马逊创始人兼首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯拥有《华盛顿邮报》。)
根据亚历西亚集团(Alethea Group)和GDI的分析，电商网站eBay是Qanon Gear的另一个热门目的地，有3000种产品与阴谋论有关。eBay没有回应记者的置评请求。Etsy曾经托管过类似的材料，本月在Facebook的镇压之后，它对阴谋论的追随者采取了行动。
与此同时，许多独立的电子商务网站提供与阴谋论有关的商品。虽然它们的所有者将自己描绘成主流平台和媒体看门人的祸根，但这些网络从与更成熟的品牌的联系中受益匪浅。
白帽运动就是这种安排的例证，这是一个由33岁的达斯汀·尼莫斯(Dustin Nemos)运营的网站集合，尼莫斯是两个孩子的父亲，他的真名是达斯汀·克里格(Dustin Krieger)。尼莫斯在北卡罗来纳州积累了很长的前科，包括与毒品有关的指控和入室行窃指控，他说他搬到了佛罗里达州西南部。在那里，他通过销售Qanon商品和参与更知名品牌的收入分享来谋生。
其中有一家应急准备企业，我的爱国者供应公司，它使用他的名字和肖像来销售紧急食品供应产品。该公司在网上享有广泛的存在，包括Facebook上超过10万名粉丝。该公司没有回应就其与尼莫斯的关系发表评论的请求。
尼莫斯说：“我们不是靠QAnon运动致富，人们想说我们是。”
尼莫斯说，他的QAnon相关书籍在亚马逊上的销售是基础性的，这有助于为他的其他一些项目提供资金。Alethea Group的分析发现，总体而言，白帽运动通过七个页面拥有超过13万Facebook用户，通过两个账户拥有约6000名Twitter用户。其中包括右翼新闻网站和其他在怀俄明州注册并自2019年以来活跃的公司。
据亚历西亚集团(Alethea Group)和GDI称，尼莫斯还寻求创建一家医疗保险公司，利用“让美国再次伟大”的口号进行交易，以及一项独立的手机服务。这两家公司援引了社交媒体网站Discorde泄露的日志。2019年的日志显示，尼莫斯正在咨询朱迪·米科维茨(Judy Mikovits)，这位名誉扫地的病毒学家今年春天在被揭穿的“Plandhemic”视频中亮相时，在极右翼社区获得了病毒式的人气。
note_en:
Months into Silicon Valley’s crackdown on the far-right QAnon conspiracy theory, dozens of websites, social media apps and e-commerce platforms remain flooded with its debunked claims, showing a durability likely to outlast next week’s presidential election — no matter the outcome.
  QAnon accounts have grown steeply on social media apps, such as Parler and Telegram, that have little content moderation. Merchants selling QAnon-themed books and T-shirts appear to be thriving, including on such major online retailers as Amazon. Even on  Twitter, one of the tech companies seeking to curb the content, accounts openly touting QAnon remain active in online political debate, especially in key states such as Florida and Texas, according to new research.
 The result is an ever-widening conspiracy-theory movement that, despite efforts by Silicon Valley to clean up its services, remains a primary fount of disinformation in the closing days of the hotly contested presidential election.
   QAnon accounts have been particularly aggressive recently in spreading unverified claims about the supposed  business dealings of Hunter Biden, the son of Democratic presidential nominee Joe Biden. The accounts also have sought to undermine confidence in mail-in voting, warning, without evidence, that the election will be “stolen” by Democrats.
 “Despite the actions of social media platforms to curtail the growth of QAnon, QAnon conspiracy theories and misinformation continue to gain traction online at an alarming rate,” said  Daniel J. Jones, a former FBI analyst and Senate investigator who now is president of Advance Democracy, which studies disinformation. “This growth has real-world consequences. We’re going to see faith in our institutions continue to erode and that’s going to make it increasingly difficult to govern.”
  Websites touting QAnon’s baseless claims, meanwhile, have rapidly adapted to efforts to knock them offline.
   The anonymous message board  8kun, which is where the mysterious figure “Q” posts cryptic clues known as “drops,” this month began using a Russian company to protect itself from online attacks after a company based in Oregon cut ties with the site, according to research by the  SITE Intelligence Group, which tracks online extremism.
 It also found that other QAnon websites have been getting similar protection from the San Francisco-based  Cloudflare. Cloudflare’s head of public policy, Alissa Starzak, said in a statement, “We do not discuss users without their permission.”
  Shortly after QAnon’s birth in October 2017 on the anonymous message board 4chan, it spread to mainstream platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, Reddit and YouTube, which propelled to much larger audiences bogus claims about satanic pedophiles running the Democratic Party. The platforms eventually cracked down — Reddit in 2018, and Facebook, Twitter and YouTube in the past few months — because of numerous violations of policies against hate speech and growing publicity about its tendency to fuel real-world violence.
   But every time a company has acted against QAnon, the conspiracy theorists found new venues for their messages while also learning to camouflage themselves on the old ones — using coded language or tweaking hashtags to avoid automated efforts to close accounts and remove content.
  This adaptability caps a banner year for QAnon, which enjoyed explosive growth by spreading misinformation about the origins and nature of the  coronavirus this past spring, portraying it as a hoax engineered by Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates and a “Satan-worshipping cabal” to confuse and control an unwitting public.
 Disinformation about the safety of vaccines, already prominent among online conspiracy theorists, emerged this year as a key QAnon narrative, as did misinformation about the racial justice protests that emerged in the aftermath of George Floyd’s killing by Minneapolis police. QAnon groups on Facebook were still growing sharply after the company’s initial effort to control it in August, with a sample of 85 accounts showing an average increase of more than 100 members, according to an  Oct. 14 report by SITE Intelligence Group.
   “Fortunately, mainstream social media companies are now aggressively moving to keep QAnon off their platforms,” said Rita Katz, executive director of SITE. “But it will require more than just well-known companies to mitigate QAnon’s threat. QAnon already has a wealth of options — if not an entire infrastructure — to fall back on.”
 In the swing states of Florida and Texas, one out of every 25 tweets on the election come from accounts affiliated with QAnon, and at least 7 percent of the responses or other engagements with Trump’s tweets came from accounts affiliated with QAnon, according to research from Advance Democracy.
 The research, relying on data from digital intelligence firm  Zignal Labs, also found that between January and mid-October, Twitter accounts affiliated with QAnon played a leading role in promoting content from far-right websites, from Breitbart to Gateway Pundit. The most active account posting on politics in Florida, meanwhile, had QAnon references in its name and profile information before being suspended on earlier this month, the analysis found.
   “We’re committed to protecting the public conversation and promoting healthy conversation on Twitter,” spokeswoman Lauren Alexander said in an email. “To date, through our work to deamplify content and accounts associated with QAnon, we have reduced impressions on QAnon-related tweets by more than 50%, meaning our users are seeing less unhealthy content on their feeds.”
 Facebook spokesman Sarah Pollack said that since expanding its policies on conspiracy theories that spur violence in August, the company has “removed about 1,700 Pages, 5,600 Groups and about 18,700 Instagram accounts representing QAnon.”
 Even as QAnon content remains popular on mainstream services, the specter of removal has pushed adherents to more hands-off platforms. Telegram, an encrypted messaging app based in Dubai that SITE Intelligence Group says “houses the majority of the international QAnon community,” has announced no enforcement against QAnon. The company did not respond to a request for comment.
   QAnon also is thriving on  Parler, a social media site popular with conservatives that has taken no action against the conspiracy theory, and Gab, whose founder, Andrew Torba, wrote that he “welcomes QAnon.” Parler did not respond to a request for comment. Gab did not offer a responsive comment to a Washington Post query.
   As QAnon messages continue to spread on various platforms, business ventures related to the conspiracy theory are also expanding — often using enforcement actions on mainstream social networking services as a selling-point. Consumer products, from T-shirts to coffee to mugs, bind adherents to the conspiracy theory just as powerfully as do memes and online catchphrases, said Lisa Kaplan, CEO and founder of Alethea Group, a counter-disinformation consultancy.
   “There’s truly an economy that’s now associated with QAnon,” Kaplan said. “We talk about it in the context of elections — for the first time there are QAnon candidates likely to be elected to Congress — but we haven’t talked enough about the individuals who are paying their mortgages and feeding their families based on QAnon merchandise sales.”
 Diatribes against the so-called deep state and cryptic clues about Trump’s impending conquest over satanic forces are interspersed on 8kun, the sprawling online site that’s home to the QAnon conspiracy theory, with more upbeat messages.
 “Stickers,” one reads, “Stickers.” Another advises: “Get Yours!” A third, similarly rendered in eye-catching yellow and black, advertises “8kun coffee.”
  The ads promote an e-commerce site operated by  Jim Watkins, a U.S. Army veteran and businessman who runs 8kun from the Philippines. Denizens of the no-frills, no-rules forum find the ads intrusive. “They are tracking us,” one wrote recently about the posts.
  The ads are also a reminder, said experts tracking the conspiracy theory, that QAnon is a moneymaking opportunity for Watkins — and for other people and groups operating platforms where the deluded philosophy is propounded. Watkins is the registered owner of the e-commerce domain linked to the ads, called Is It Wet Yet. The site is connected to a political action committee he founded in February called Disarm the Deep State, which enjoys a presence on both Facebook and Twitter and describes itself as the “first Super PAC dedicated to removing shadow government actors.”
 “It’s obvious self-dealing,” said Fredrick Brennan, a software developer who founded 8kun’s precursor, called 8chan, in 2013 but has since called for it to be shut down, directing criticism at the site’s new owner that recently led  Watkins to bring a “cyberlibel” case against him. “He knows he’s in total control of Q, whether or not he writes the posts himself, so he’s been able to turn it into an industry,” Brennan said.
  The “profiteering,” said Brennan, extends beyond Watkins and has intensified because of the recent crackdowns on social media. Following enforcement actions by the major technology companies, the conspiracy theory has been sustained in large part by a robust digital marketplace for goods branded with its iconography.
 The theory’s evangelists criticize Big Pharma, and then promote alternative medicines to those whom they’ve convinced. They warn of an impending social collapse, and then peddle “ prepper” equipment for newly minted survivalists.
 QAnon marketplaces bridge mainstream and fringe online platforms. The most popular site is Amazon, with more than 8,000 individual QAnon-branded products, according to an analysis conducted by Alethea Group and the Global Disinformation Index.
 Amazon Books has at least 30 different QAnon-related texts, many of which end up on top-seller lists for religious philosophy, controversial religious knowledge and other obscure genres.
 “Amazon is the one helping QAnon influencers make money, and it might even be more important than Facebook,” Brennan said. “They need payment processes as much as they need social media. If they don’t have payment processes, it doesn’t matter how viral they are — they can’t collect donations.”
 An Amazon spokeswoman, Mary Kate McCarthy, declined to comment. (Amazon founder and CEO Jeff Bezos owns The Washington Post.)
 The e-commerce site eBay, which did not respond to a request for comment, is another popular destination for QAnon gear, with 3,000 products related to the conspiracy theory, according to the analysis by Alethea Group and the GDI. Etsy, which once hosted similar material, acted against the conspiracy theory’s adherents this month, following the crackdown by Facebook.
  Meanwhile, numerous independent e-commerce sites offer merchandise related to the conspiracy theory. While their owners present themselves as the bane of mainstream platforms and media gatekeepers, these networks benefit crucially from their associations with more established brands.
 Exemplifying the arrangement is the White Hat Movement, a collection of sites operated by Dustin Nemos, a 33-year-old father of two whose real name is Dustin Krieger. Having amassed a lengthy rap sheet in North Carolina, including drug-related and burglary charges, Nemos said he relocated to southwest Florida. There, he makes a living by selling QAnon merchandise and participating in revenue shares with more prominent brands.
 Among these is an emergency preparedness business, My Patriot Supply, which uses his name and likeness to sell emergency food supply products. The business, which enjoys a vast online presence, including more than 100,000 followers on Facebook, did not respond to a request for comment about its ties to Nemos.
 “We’re not getting rich off the QAnon movement, which people want to say we are,” Nemos said.
 Sales of his QAnon-related book on Amazon were foundational, Nemos said, helping to fund some of his other ventures. In all, the White Hat Movement has a reach of more 130,000 Facebook users through seven pages and about 6,000 Twitter users on two accounts, Alethea Group’s analysis found. These include right-wing news sites and other corporations registered in Wyoming and active since 2019.
 Nemos has also sought to create a health insurance company trading on the “Make America Great Again” slogan, as well as an independent cellphone service, according to Alethea Group and GDI, which cited leaked logs from the social media site  Discord. The 2019 logs suggest Nemos was consulting Judy Mikovits, the discredited virologist who gained viral popularity in far-right communities when she was featured this spring in the debunked “Plandemic” video.
title: 在社交媒体打压之后，QAnon内容在亚马逊以及Parler和Telegram等平台上继续蓬勃发展，但有限度地
title_en: Following social media crackdowns, QAnon content is continuing to thrive on Amazon and platforms like Parler and Telegram with limited moderation

微软正准备在2021年为Windows10进行一次重大的操作系统更新，有消息称，这将给Windows UI带来重大的设计更新。我被告知，微软正计划更新许多顶级用户界面，如开始菜单、操作中心，甚至文件资源管理器，这些界面都采用了更新的现代设计、更好的动画和新功能。
这个UI项目在内部代号为太阳谷，预计将作为定于2021年假日季发布的Windows10.Cobalt&34；的一部分发货。内部文档将该项目描述为重振和现代化Windows桌面体验，以满足客户在由其他现代化和轻量级平台驱动的世界中的期望。
Windows10在过去几年中基本保持不变，其设计或功能集几乎没有变化。在过去的五年里，市场上的许多其他平台都经历了彻底的重新设计或UI刷新，虽然Windows10在引入流畅设计后经历了一些小的设计迭代，但我们并没有看到它的UI有重大的更新或重新考虑。
太阳谷项目似乎是由Windows Devices and Experience团队带头的，该团队由首席产品官Panos Panay领导，他早在2月份就负责该部门。微软在5月份宣布，该公司将在2021年对Windows10进行再投资，我的消息来源称，太阳谷就是这一再投资的结果。
现在确定太阳谷将更新什么还为时过早，但消息人士称，预计会有新的开始菜单和操作中心体验，很可能基于Windows 10X上的相同体验，但为桌面量身定做。微软还在开发一个使用现代代码构建的更新任务栏，并为遗留文件资源管理器改进用户界面。
对于平板电脑用户，我告诉他们，更好的动画和更流畅的体验是有可能的。我们已经知道微软正在重新设计触摸键盘和表情符号选择器，因为这些变化已经在Windows Insider Dev Channel中进行了。微软还将继续在整个用户界面(包括应用程序窗口和其他外壳区域)中进行舍入。
消息人士还说，预计WinUI将在整个Windows Shell和收件箱应用程序中得到更广泛的采用，这应该会带来微妙而又改进的设计变化。此外，在使用Windows10的黑色主题时，预计会有更多的传统UI区域获得深色模式支持，从而使Windows UI的外观和感觉更加一致。
据我所知，这个更新的设计将是流畅设计的演变，很可能仍然被称为流畅设计。微软并没有在太阳谷引入一种新的设计语言，它只是将当前的设计语言更新和重新聚焦于桌面，并试图在整个操作系统中更一致地应用它，这对传统的Windows桌面来说是一个巨大的壮举。
重要的是要强调，微软可以在从现在到明年更新发货之间的任何时间削减或推迟这些计划。其中一些计划很可能无法进入最终产品，因为这正是Windows OS开发的本质，也是微软没有提前宣布这些计划的原因。但是，这些都是微软明年想要交付给Windows10客户的东西。
微软希望在2021年6月结束的Cobalt开发学期结束前完成大部分工作。然后，微软将RTM一个版本，将其发送给OEM，并开始在Beta Channel中作为指定版本进行测试。更新本身直到秋季才会向公众推出，很可能是LCU(最新累积更新)，它位于最新功能和修复的顶端。
如果微软能够实现与太阳谷的合作计划，这将是迄今为止我们看到的最大的Windows10用户界面更新。此前，Windows10已经被搁置了长达三年之久。Panos Panay希望人们从对Windows的需求转变为对Windows的喜爱，而直观和设计驱动的现代更新界面是一个很好的开端。
与从Windows 7过渡到Windows 8不同，有了太阳谷，个人电脑用户仍将熟悉Windows 10。我还被告知，对于某些功能，用户将能够在新旧体验之间切换，让用户有选择的余地，而不是强迫他们。太阳谷的目的是改进和更新熟悉的Windows UX，而不是从根本上改变它。
目前，您对微软的太阳谷项目有何看法？请在评论中让我们知道。
note_en:
Microsoft is preparing a major OS update for  Windows 10 in 2021 that sources say will bring with it a significant design refresh to the Windows UI. I'm told that Microsoft is planning to update many top-level user interfaces such as the Start menu, Action Center, and even File Explorer, with refreshed modern designs, better animations, and new features.
 This UI project is codenamed "Sun Valley" internally and is expected to ship as part of the Windows 10 "Cobalt" release scheduled for the holiday 2021 season. Internal documentation describes the project as "reinvigorating" and modernizing the Windows desktop experience to keep up with customer expectation in a world driven by other modern and lightweight platforms.
  Windows 10 has remained much the same these last few years, with little to no changes in its design or feature set. Many other platforms on the market have gone through entire redesigns or UI refreshes in the last five years, and while Windows 10 has gone through minor design iterations with the introduction of Fluent Design, we've not seen a significant refresh or rethinking of its UI.
 The Sun Valley project appears to be spearheaded by the  Windows Devices and Experiences team, lead by Chief Product Officer Panos Panay, who  took charge of said division back in February. Microsoft announced in May that  the company would be "reinvesting" in Windows 10 in the 2021 timeframe, and my sources say that Sun Valley is the result of that reinvestment.
  It's still too early to nail down exactly what will be updated with Sun Valley, but sources have said to expect new Start menu and Action Center experiences, likely based on those same experiences found on Windows 10X, but tailored for desktop. Microsoft is also working on an updated Taskbar built with modern code, and an improved UI for the legacy File Explorer.
 For tablet users, I'm told that better animations and a more "fluid experience" is on the cards. We already know that Microsoft is redesigning the touch keyboard and emoji picker, as those changes are already live in the Windows Insider Dev Channel. Microsoft will also continue its escapade of rounding-off corners throughout the UI, including app windows and other shell areas.
 Sources also say to expect a wider adoption of WinUI throughout the Windows Shell and in-box apps, which should provide subtle yet improved design changes. In addition, more legacy UI areas are expected to get dark mode support in an effort to make the Windows UI look and feel more consistent when using Windows 10's dark theme.
 I understand that this refreshed design will be an evolution of Fluent Design, and likely still called Fluent Design. Microsoft isn't introducing a new design language with Sun Valley, it's simply refreshing and refocusing the current one on desktop, and trying to apply it more consistently throughtout the OS, a big feat for the legacy Windows desktop.
  It's important to stress that Microsoft can cut or delay these plans at any time between now and when the update ships next year. It's likely that some of these plans won't make it to the final product, as that's just the nature of Windows OS development, and the reason why Microsoft doesn't announce these plans ahead of time. But, these are the things Microsoft  wants to deliver to Windows 10 customers next year.
 Microsoft is hoping to have most of this work done by the end of the Cobalt development semester, which wraps up in June 2021. Microsoft will then RTM a build, ship that off to OEMs and begin testing it in the Beta Channel as a designated release. The update itself won't roll out to the public until the fall, likely with an LCU (latest cumulative update) which sits on top with last-minute features and fixes.
 If Microsoft is able to pull off its plans with Sun Valley, this will be the biggest Windows 10 UI refresh we've seen so far, coming after three long years of Windows 10 sitting on the back burner. Panos Panay wants people to go from  needing Windows to loving Windows, and a modern refreshed interface that's intuitive and design-driven is a great start.
 With Sun Valley, Windows 10 will still be familiar to PC users, unlike the transition from Windows 7 to Windows 8. I'm also told that for some features, users will be able to switch between the new and old experiences, giving users a choice rather than forcing it upon them. Sun Valley is all about improving and modernizing the familiar Windows UX, and not radically changing it up.
 For now, what are your thoughts on Microsoft's Sun Valley project? Let us know in the comments.
title: 来源：Microsoft计划在2021年进行广泛的Windows 10更新，更新许多顶级UI的设计，包括Start和File Explorer
title_en: Sources: Microsoft is planning an extensive Windows 10 update for 2021 with design refreshes for many top-level UIs, including Start and File Explorer

在PlayStation5于11月12日发布之前，索尼推出了针对Android和iOS的PlayStation App的重大更新。此次更新引入了全新的用户界面、语音聊天支持、原生的PlayStation Store集成，以及索尼声称的更为精致的体验。
重新设计的PlayStation App正在全球范围内推出，并适用于Android 6.0及更高版本。以下是今日更新的主要变化：
PlayStation App有一个更新的用户界面，包括一个刷新的主屏幕，用户可以看到他们的朋友在玩什么，还可以访问最近玩过的游戏的详细信息。
PS Messages应用程序已经集成到PS应用程序中，可以更轻松地与朋友保持联系。因此，PS Messages应用程序将停产。
用户可以通过PS App创建党组，还可以与最多15人进行语音聊天。
PS商店现在是PS应用程序的原生版本，为用户提供更快、更流畅的购物体验。用户可以直接将游戏和附加组件远程下载到他们的PS4和PS5。
当PS5面市时，用户将能够直接从PS应用程序远程启动游戏、管理存储等功能。
作为更新的一部分，索尼表示，它正在调查有关8.00更新中对各方所做更改的反馈。索尼表示：“今天没有最新消息可以分享，但感谢你说出你的担忧--我们正在倾听和评估。”
你需要一个PlayStation账户才能充分利用PS App的重大更新，从今天开始就可以使用了。
note_en:
Ahead of the PlayStation 5’s launch on November 12, Sony has introduced a big update to its PlayStation App for Android and iOS. The update introduces a completely revamped UI, voice chat support, native PlayStation Store integration, and what Sony claims is a much more refined experience.
  The redesigned PlayStation App is rolling out globally and is available for Android 6.0 and later. Here are the key changes in today’s update:
 The PlayStation App features an updated user interface, including a refreshed home screen that allows users to see what their friends are playing and also access details about recently played games.
 The PS Messages app has been integrated into the PS App, making it easier to keep in contact with friends. As a result, the PS Messages app will be discontinued.
 Users can create party groups from the PS App and also voice chat with up to 15 people.
 The PS Store is now native to the PS App, providing users with a faster, smoother shopping experience. Users can remotely download games and add-ons directly to their PS4 and PS5.
 When the PS5 becomes available, users will be able to remotely launch games, manage storage, and more right from the PS App.
  As part of the update, Sony said it’s looking into feedback regarding changes made to Parties in the 8.00 update. “There are no updates to share today, but thanks for speaking up about your concerns — we’re listening and evaluating,” Sony said.
 You need a  PlayStation account in order to take full advantage of the PS App’s big update, which is available starting today.
title: 索尼更新了适用于iOS和Android的PlayStation App，引入了应用内语音聊天支持、改进的UI、PlayStation Store集成等
title_en: Sony updates its PlayStation App for iOS and Android, introducing in-app voice chat support, a revamped UI, PlayStation Store integration, and more

note:
去年深秋的一天，Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西坐在公司旧金山屋顶甲板上的沙发上，深究一个问题。
在他旁边的是该公司的顶级律师维贾亚·加德(Vijaya Gadde)。距离2020年美国大选只有一年的时间，Twitter的高管担心该公司正在陷入它在2016年帮助加剧的混乱局面，当时政治竞选和俄罗斯造谣艺术家通过精准的社交媒体广告给系统注入了如此多的混乱，以至于世界民主机构几乎跟不上。
Twitter增加了新的透明度规则，要求广告买家披露他们是谁。这还不够。它还能做什么呢？
加德向多尔西推销了一个激进的解决方案：也许Twitter应该停止销售政治广告。
这是一个大胆的想法-没有其他主要的美国平台简单地禁止政治广告-多尔西也没有立即被出售。首先，该公司围绕着主持自由流动的公开对话的承诺建立了自己。Gadde-推特官方的法律、政策和信托主管-敦促她的案件，她在大楼内有盟友支持这个想法，包括推特的信任和安全团队的负责人。几天内，多尔西签署了这一想法，宣布于2019年10月30日在全球范围内禁止政治广告，并在一条11条推文中详细阐述了该公司的理由。
我们已经决定在全球范围内停止推特上的所有政治广告。我们认为，政治信息的影响应该是赢得的，而不是收买的。为什么？…的几个原因。🧵。
-杰克(@jack)2019年10月30日。
对于那些了解Twitter内部运作的人来说，这是加德影响力不断上升的又一迹象。加德是一名人脉广泛、倾向自由派的律师，他帮助推动公司对用户可以发表的言论和帖子进行了更严格的监管。推特的新规则，从广告禁令到删除有争议的新冠肺炎推文，已经在硅谷产生了涟漪，并在美国政坛引起了巨大的反弹，许多人-特别是保守派-现在认为推特是不友好的领域。
她还帮助推特的政策与Facebook截然相反，Facebook的首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)曾宣布，私人公司“审查”政客是错误的。
加德是导致推特取缔所有人的政策方法的首席设计师，从日常骚扰者到骄傲的男孩，再到唐纳德·特朗普总统，她一直站在前面为其辩护，认为这一转变作为公司战略是有意义的。今年7月，加德在旧金山的家中接受电话采访时谈到了禁止政治广告的决定：“除了说‘这对我们作为一家公司来说是正确的事情’之外，什么都不是。”
两周前，当“纽约邮报”(The New York Post)发表了一篇报道和电子邮件图片，声称在2015年，乌克兰高管付钱给乔·拜登(Joe Biden)的儿子亨特(Hunter)，让他安排与时任副总统的会面时，Gadde成为了该公司与政治右翼的最新争斗的中心。随着这个故事开始受到关注，特别是在急于质疑拜登道德的保守派中，Twitter迅速而几乎没有透明度地阻止用户链接到它。那天晚上，多尔西道歉了。第二天进行公共清理的人是Gadde，他在一系列推文中表示，Twitter正在彻底修改它在此案中援引的“黑客材料政策”。多尔西现在正在为周三出现在参议院商务委员会(Senate Commerce Committee)做准备，之前安排的一次会议现在预计将变成“华盛顿邮报”争议的公开重播。加德正在为多尔西的听证会做准备。
虽然加德在Twitter之外相当低调，但在公司内部，她因在Twitter最关键的时刻与公司首席执行官关系密切而闻名。当多尔西在2019年与唐纳德·特朗普总统会面时，加德和他们一起在椭圆形办公室。她在印度与多尔西一起会见达赖喇嘛。她陪同他前往华盛顿，与立法者交谈，并会见记者。在Twitter总部内，加德和多尔西在隔壁的空间里工作。一位推特官员说：“我无法想象杰克看着她说‘不’的世界。”
一位前Twitter官员对这一动态进行了不同的转折：“当谈到开战时，杰克是发出命令的总统。维贾亚是参谋长联席会议主席。“。
加德管理着大约350000人，从事从处理有争议的新冠肺炎事实到反击政府的方方面面的工作，包括反对土耳其的一项服务禁令，以及在美国就推特是否可以根据其索取用户数据的要求披露政府封口令的细节而提起诉讼，这起案件持续了六年之久。她一直在推动技术改革，允许公司尝试规避钝化武力禁令和封锁，比如在攻击性推文上贴上警告标签，限制转发以阻止不良信息的传播。(5月下旬，当Twitter在邮寄选票上标记出特朗普的两条误导性推文时，加德正在休育儿假，但她对他被标记的政策负有最终责任。)。
随着Twitter越来越多地放弃对每周数十亿条推文的不插手做法，它为自己赢得了赞扬和批评的令人兴奋的组合。众议员亚历山德里亚·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯(Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez)称推特的广告政策禁令是“一个好决定”，但许多左翼活动家认为，该平台仍然允许太多的仇恨言论和恐吓性言论。民主党和共和党的竞选团队都批评广告禁令是对经常依赖廉价社交媒体广告的失败者的打击。
联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)共和党成员布伦丹·卡尔(Brendan Carr)指责Twitter是企业的诱饵和转变，它早期的做法引诱了广泛的政治言论，一旦有了足够的影响力，就会应用加州自由派的过滤器。“当他们寻求积累权力和用户时，他们毫不犹豫地说，‘我们代表自由言论党的言论自由派别，’”他说。“然后突然之间，一旦你根据这些表示获得了令人难以置信的规模，你就会转身。”
保守派倡导团体媒体研究中心(Media Research Center)的创始人布伦特·博泽尔(Brent Bozell)猜测，Twitter押注的是乔·拜登(Joe Biden)获胜：“如果特朗普获胜，共和党人保持对参议院的控制，”他说，“我认为他们将为此付出地狱般的代价。”其中一些地狱可能已经开始：在“纽约邮报”(New York Post)引发争议之后，自称为科技盟友的加州众议院少数党领袖凯文·麦卡锡(Kevin McCarthy)表示，“是时候废除”这项数十年来的法律，这项法律赋予Twitter等网站宝贵的保护，使其不必为用户发布的内容承担责任。
不管有什么风险，Gadde帮助推动的变化已经波及整个硅谷：Spotify在Twitter暂停两个月后暂停了政治广告；就连随心所欲的公告牌Reddit也打击了仇恨言论。
然而，一个值得注意的竞争对手对Twitter最近的举动不屑一顾，认为它只不过是公关手段。“他们没有仇恨言论的政策，他们有特朗普的政策，”Facebook的一名高管嗤之以鼻，他表示，Twitter因在几条备受瞩目的总统推文上贴上标签而获得了过高的赞誉。
“显然是错误的，”加德称之为这种描述。她还强烈驳斥了有关推特的枪击行为是受党派政治驱使的批评。
首席执行官杰克·多尔西是Twitter的代名词。但当谈到可能影响选举的政策时，维贾亚·加德(Vijaya Gadde)是你从未听说过的最重要的硅谷高管。Politico的南希·斯科拉报道了推特上最重大决策背后的女性。
维贾亚·加德(Vijaya Gadde)是在巧妙地引导Twitter通过浑浊的水域，还是永久性地疏远了一大批美国人，包括一些最终可能对Twitter的未来拥有发言权的华盛顿人？加德本人并没有退缩，她希望帮助边缘化的人创造一个更舒适、更有能力的世界。她是人道主义援助组织Mercy Corps的董事会成员，此外，她和其他女性Twitter退伍军人运营着一家风险基金，帮助女性在创业世界中崛起。
Twitter越是乐于从一个不可知的平台转变为一个对什么是“健康的”对话有着特殊想法的平台，问题就越多：你真的能阻止一个全球社交网络变成一个污水池，而不变成语言警察吗？
加德说：“过去九年来，我一直在做的就是试图弄清楚这条路。”而就在选举日之前，这条道路将推特带到了一个令人担忧的地方。
Gadde在7月份的一次采访中说：“我真的看不到一个简单的解决方案来解决如何在世界各地大规模调整内容的问题。”“会有错误，会有修正，也会有不一致之处。”
“华盛顿邮报”的报道是一个完美的例子，说明了Twitter的新的、更具限制性的方法是多么困难，特别是在处理快速移动的新闻时。10月14日，Twitter迅速采取行动，阻止用户发布一篇令人震惊的文章的链接，这篇文章虽然未经证实，但涉及民主党总统候选人的儿子亨特·拜登(Hunter Biden)的不当行为。正如Twitter最终所描述的那样，令人担忧的是：故事中心的材料被黑客入侵，并泄露了故事中人物的个人信息。距离选举日只有20天了，但社交媒体公司对恶作剧处于一触即发的警戒状态；执法部门一直在警告2020年大选面临的威胁，包括“黑客和泄密”行动。
Twitter的禁令，包括锁定一名发布这篇报道的知名Politico记者的账户，转移了人们对Facebook的关注。Facebook不同寻常地领先于Twitter，宣布将在事实核查人员查看报道的同时减少报道的分发。联邦通信委员会的卡尔说，“对Facebook来说，最好的事情就是推特告诉它，‘拿着我的啤酒。’”
在Twitter，个人执法决定是沿着链条向下做出的，但第二天，加德就得站出来，在她的Twitter账户@Vijaya上解释Twitter是如何在反弹中改变路线的：“在反思了这个反馈后，我们决定对政策和我们的执行方式做出改变，”她写道。她解释说，Twitter正在逐步减少其“被黑客攻击的材料”政策，尽管其他规则，比如对发布个人信息的限制，仍然适用。
一些人不相信加德的解释，即最初的封锁是对现有政策的直接应用。保守派出版物“人类事件”(Human Events)主编威尔·张伯伦(Will Chamberlain)在推特上写道，“嗨@Vijaya！你有什么证据证明亨特·拜登的材料被黑了？这似乎是援引你们的‘黑客材料政策’的先决条件。“。她对帖子链接违反了Twitter其他哪些政策的细微差别在愤怒中消失了。当用户发现他们仍然无法发布帖子链接时，许多人开始怀疑Twitter是不是在胡说八道。
在“华盛顿邮报”的报道首次发布两天后，也就是距离选举日只有18天的时候，Twitter完全改弦易辙：该公司表示，“华盛顿邮报”的报道中包含的任何个人信息都已经公开，所以用户现在可以自由地在Twitter上发布它。
多尔西是对Twitter处理这一情况的不满人士之一，他在推特上表示，该公司就Post的情况进行的沟通“不是很好”，而且在没有告诉用户原因的情况下笨手笨脚地屏蔽了这篇报道，这是“不可接受的”。
然而，到那时，共和党人已经抓住它作为一种模式的一部分，在这种模式下，Twitter的边缘案件似乎总是与保守派竞争。为什么用户可以根据特朗普泄露的财务记录在推特上发布“纽约时报”的一篇报道的链接，而不能在“纽约邮报”上发布一篇关于亨特·拜登(Hunter Biden)电子邮件的报道？参议员泰德·克鲁兹(德克萨斯州共和党人)。称这是推特影响即将到来的总统选举的一次明显而透明的尝试。共和党全国委员会向联邦选举委员会提出申诉。参议院承诺将在听证会上弄清这件事的真相。在Twitter和Facebook取缔“华盛顿邮报”报道的第二天，FCC主席宣布，他的机构将启动正式程序，以“澄清”通信法如何适用于在线平台-这被广泛视为对Twitter及其同行迄今运营的责任自由的威胁。
Gadde现年45岁，身高5英尺4英寸，以前曾帮助Twitter渡过急流。2011年，她在另一个关键时刻来到这里。这家成立5年的公司当时还是一家杂乱无章的初创公司，仍然靠唾沫和管道胶带维系在一起，它的非官方吉祥物“失败鲸”(Failure Whale)是一种可爱的卡通动物，每当其紧张的服务器再次出现故障时，它就会出现。但作为一个平台，Twitter正在迅速成为全球活动中的一股力量-它的5000万用户遍布世界各地，它在最近的伊朗抗议活动和阿拉伯之春革命中扮演的角色，让人们思考，这个为科技业内人士提供的略微愚蠢的聊天服务是否会有什么严肃的事情。
加德曾在硅谷声势浩大的威尔逊·桑西尼·古德里奇·罗萨蒂律师事务所(Wilson Sonsini Goodrich&amp；Rosati)工作，在Twitter计划上市之际，她的工作是避免其他高调发布时出现的混乱局面。用“华尔街日报”(The Wall Street Journal)的话说，Facebook 2012年的首次公开募股(IPO)是一场“惨败”，从技术故障到一些投资者所说的隐瞒关键信息，一切都受到了影响。谷歌2004年的首次公开募股(IPO)使用了一种未经测试的模式，让投资者压低了这家搜索引擎的股票。推特不想要同样的尴尬。到那时，加德已经在那里工作了两年，从事并购工作，公司高管和董事会已经开始将她视为掌舵的稳扎稳打的人。聘请加德的推特当时的法律总顾问亚历克斯·麦吉利夫雷(Alex MacGillivray)说，公司的最高层“喜欢”他们在律师身上看到的东西。
推特的IPO进展顺利。高盛(Goldman Sachs)的首席银行家在推特上简单地写道：“嘘！”Gadde之前刚刚被任命为总法律顾问，她正在发展自己的声誉，成为前Twitter全球公共政策主管科林·克罗威尔(Colin Crowell)所说的“每一场风暴中心的平静”。
随着公司的成长和Twitter业务的稳定，另一个因素变得越来越重要：社交媒体的道德和影响力。多年来，联合创始人比兹·斯通(Biz Stone)在2011年的一篇博客文章中总结了这一可行的假设：“推文必须源源不断。”当时的政策是，除非推文违反法律或明显是垃圾信息，否则不会发布推文，因为他们认为，对糟糕的推文最好的解药就是让它们被好的推文淹没。
2015年10月，Twitter联合创始人杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)回归，此前他曾被董事会赶下台。通过创建支付公司Square的经历，他变得成熟起来，他接受了经营一家使命驱动型公司的想法，这也是一项不错的业务。Twitter既需要广告商，也需要新用户，但一些在线世界对这个平台已经变得免费的局面感到畏缩。
当谈到重新考虑自由放任的方法时，多尔西有一个现成的盟友加德。六个月前，她曾在“华盛顿邮报”的版面上辩称，Twitter必须停止以个人的推文自由为祭坛，牺牲服务的集体安全。“平衡这一信念的两个方面--欢迎不同的观点，同时保护我们的用户--需要保持警惕，并愿意做出艰难的选择，”她写道，然后发表了道歉声明：“这是我们近年来有时未能实现的理想。”
虽然Facebook在很大程度上仍然是马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)的节目，但多尔西的风格有所不同。他将自己的时间分配给Square，为其他高管在Twitter上发挥日常影响力留下了相当大的空间。公司内部人士和密切观察人士表示，他愿意听从其他公司领导人，通常是加德-加德在Twitter近十年的工作经历使她成为一家高管级别大幅更替的公司里根深蒂固的资深人士。苏珊·贝内施(Susan Benesch)是一名学者，作为她领导的危险演讲项目的一部分，她曾与Twitter密切合作：“甚至很难理解她是如何做到这么多的。她有几份既困难又耗时的工作。“。
多尔西在电子邮件中说：“Vijaya为我们的工作带来了关键的平衡。她对我们的目标深信不疑，并预见到我们未来将面临的所有挑战。我非常感谢她孜孜不倦地工作，让我们每天都变得更好，确保我们始终考虑我们在世界上的角色和影响。“。(推特上的一名官员说，多尔西很少对加德说不：“如果她不在那里，我认为人们会带着更多的东西逍遥法外。”)
2016年大选后，Twitter受到华盛顿许多人的谴责，因为它似乎让错误信息泛滥。多尔西最终在华盛顿赢得了赞誉，因为他正面面对投诉，在国会作证时，加德就坐在他身后。但Twitter仍然面临批评，称该服务几乎是一个无底深渊，充斥着种族主义攻击、性别歧视的诽谤、反犹的堆积如山和堆狗，这些往往会失控，而目标抱怨徒劳。慢慢地，犹豫不决地，该公司开始推出新的政策-比如对滥用采取更强硬的立场-并执行其他政策，比如清除网站上的虚假账户。
2018年8月，当Twitter为什么没有禁止右翼挑战者亚历克斯·琼斯(Alex Jones)时，这一切都还悬而未决，Facebook、苹果和YouTube已经采取了这一步骤。“纽约客”那周的每日漫画以琼斯的素描为特色，上面写道：“别担心，我们仍然欢迎你在推特和地铁等对阴谋者友好的平台上使用。”同一天，多尔西在推特上写道：“肯定不满意我们的政策。他们需要进化。做那份工作。“。
然后，Twitter内部似乎突然出现了一些东西。次月，该平台禁止了琼斯，次年7月宣布了一项关于非人性化语言的新政策，旨在解决此类言论的线下危害，然后在10月份继续禁止政治广告。随后，它又出台了一项针对所谓被操纵的媒体的政策，比如深度造假。2020年春天，随着新冠肺炎疫情的爆发，推特推出了一系列新的变化，旨在阻止有关该病毒的危险信息的传播。
Twitter的道路与其规模更大的竞争对手Facebook选择的道路形成了鲜明对比。扎克伯格一再表示，Facebook不应该成为世界上的“真相仲裁者”。近年来，扎克伯格表明了一种更接近言论自由-绝对主义的立场，这一点在去年乔治敦大学(Georgetown University)的一次演讲中进行了详细阐述，并得到了前英国刑警组织负责人尼克·克莱格(Nick Clegg)的支持。克莱格曾在谈到Facebook时表示，“审查或扼杀政治言论将与我们的宗旨不符。”
在Facebook内部，一些高管指出，Twitter需要巡逻的内容流要少得多。Twitter约有4900名员工；多尔西不愿透露其中有多少人或外部承包商从事内容审核工作，部分原因是这是一个根据世界事件而变化的数字。Facebook有大约45,000名员工，该公司并不否认有报道称，该公司在全球有15,000名员工作为承包商从事内容审核工作。
Gadde很快表示，内容审核是一项团队工作，也是一项极其困难的工作。“语言是人类学能想象到的最复杂的事情之一，”加德在8月份的一封电子邮件中写道，“我们的规则必须符合这种复杂性。”
2019年3月，这种复杂性成为乔主持的一集播客的核心内容。
.
note_en:
On a late fall day last year, Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey sat down on a couch on the company’s San Francisco roof deck and dug into a problem.
 Next to him was the company’s top lawyer, Vijaya Gadde. The 2020 U.S. election was barely a year out and Twitter executives were worried the company was steering into the exact mess it had helped fuel in 2016, when political campaigns and Russian disinformation artists had pumped so much chaos into the system through precision-targeted social-media ads that the world’s democratic institutions could barely keep up.
  Twitter had added new transparency rules, making ad buyers disclose who they were. It wasn’t enough. What more could it do?
 Gadde pitched Dorsey on a radical idea for a fix: Maybe Twitter should just, well, stop selling political ads.
 It was a bold idea—no other major American platform had simply banned political ads—and Dorsey wasn’t immediately sold. For one thing, the company had built itself around a commitment to hosting a free-flowing public conversation. Gadde—officially Twitter’s head of legal, policy and trust—pressed her case, and she had allies on the idea inside the building, including the head of Twitter’s trust and safety team. Within days Dorsey signed off on the idea, announcing a global ban on political ads on October 30, 2019, in  an 11-tweet thread detailing the company’s reasoning.
 We’ve made the decision to stop all political advertising on Twitter globally. We believe political message reach should be earned, not bought. Why? A few reasons…🧵
— jack (@jack)  October 30, 2019
 For those who know the inner workings of Twitter, it was another sign of the rising influence of Gadde, the connected, liberal-leaning lawyer who has helped drive the company to more heavily regulate what users can say and post. Twitter’s new rules, from the ad ban to its deletion of controversial Covid-19 tweets, have rippled through Silicon Valley and caused huge blowback in American politics, where many—especially conservatives—now see Twitter as unfriendly territory.
 She has also helped put Twitter’s policy squarely at odds with Facebook, whose CEO Mark Zuckerberg had declared it wrong for private companies to “censor” politicians.
  Gadde was a lead architect of the policy approach that led Twitter to clamp down on everyone from everyday harassers to the Proud Boys to President Donald Trump, and she’s been out front in defending it, arguing that the shift makes sense as corporate strategy. Said Gadde in an interview by phone from her home in San Francisco in July, of the decision to ban political ads: “It wasn’t about anything other than, ‘This is the right thing to do for us as a company.’”
 Two weeks ago, Gadde emerged at the center of the company’s latest tangle with the political right, when the New York Post published a story and images of emails purporting to show that in 2015, Ukrainian executives had essentially paid Joe Biden’s son Hunter to arrange a meeting with the then-vice president.  As the story began to get traction, especially among conservatives eager to cast doubt on Biden’s ethics, Twitter quickly and with little transparency blocked users from linking to it. That evening,  Dorsey apologized. The person who did the public cleanup the next day was Gadde, who  in a series of tweets said Twitter was overhauling the “hacked materials policy” it had invoked in the case. Dorsey is now girding for a Wednesday appearance before the Senate Commerce Committee, a previously scheduled session now expected to turn into a public re-airing of the Post controversy. Gadde is prepping Dorsey for the hearing.
  Though she keeps a fairly low profile outside Twitter, Gadde is known within the company for being close to the company’s CEO during Twitter’s highest-stakes moments. When Dorsey met with President Donald Trump in 2019, Gadde was in the Oval Office with them. She was  at Dorsey’s side to meet the Dalai Lama in India. She’s accompanied him to Washington to speak with legislators and  meet with journalists. Inside Twitter’s headquarters, Gadde and Dorsey work from spaces next door to each other. Said one Twitter official: “I can’t imagine a world where Jack looks at her and says, ‘No.’”
 A former Twitter official put a different twist on the dynamic: “When it comes to going to war, Jack is the president who gives the order. Vijaya is the chairman of the Joint Chiefs.”
 Gadde oversees about 350 people, working on everything from handling disputed Covid-19 facts to pushing back on governments, including  fighting a ban on the service in Turkey and  litigating an ongoing six-year legal case in the U.S. over whether Twitter can reveal details of government gag orders on its requests for users’ data. She’s pushed for technical changes that allow the company to try sidestepping blunt-force bans and blocks, like  attaching warning labels to offensive tweets and  limiting retweets to stop the spread of bad information. (Gadde was on parental leave when, in late May, Twitter flagged a pair of Trump’s misleading tweets on mail-in ballots, but she’s ultimately responsible for the policies he was flagged under.)
 As Twitter has increasingly ditched its hands-off approach to the billions  of tweets that flow across it each week, it has earned itself a heady mix of both praise and criticism. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez called Twitter’s ad policy ban “a good call,” but many activists on the left think the platform still lets through far too much hate speech and threatening language. And plenty of both Democratic and Republican campaigns criticized the ad ban as a blow to underdogs, who often rely on inexpensive social media advertising.
 Brendan Carr, a Republican member of the Federal Communications Commission, accuses Twitter of a corporate bait and switch, luring in a wide range of political speech with its early approach and then applying a California-liberal filter once it had enough clout. “When they were looking to amass power and users, they had no qualms saying, ‘We represent the free speech wing of the free speech party,’” he said. “Then all of the sudden, once you have obtained just incredible scale based on those representations, you turn heel.”
 Brent Bozell, founder of the conservative advocacy group Media Research Center, guesses Twitter is betting on a Joe Biden win: “Should Trump win and Republicans retain control of the Senate,” he said, “I think they’re going to have hell to pay for this.” Some of that hell may already be starting: In the wake of the New York Post controversy, House Minority Leader Kevin McCarthy, a Californian who has styled himself a tech ally,  said “it is time to scrap” the decades-old law that grants websites like Twitter valuable protection against liability for what users post.
 Whatever the risks,  the changes Gadde helped drive have rippled through Silicon Valley: Spotify suspended political ads two months after Twitter did; even Reddit, the freewheeling bulletin board, has cracked down on hate speech.
 One notable  rival, however, disdains Twitter’s recent moves as little more than PR ploys. “They haven’t got a hate speech policy, they have a Trump policy,” sniffed a Facebook executive, who says Twitter has gotten outsize credit for slapping labels on a few high-profile presidential tweets.
 “Patently false,” Gadde calls that characterization. She also strongly rejects the critique that Twitter’s shot-calling is driven by partisan politics.
 CEO Jack Dorsey is synonymous with Twitter. But when it comes to the policies that could affect the election, Vijaya Gadde is the most important Silicon Valley executive you've never heard of. POLITICO's Nancy Scola reports on the woman behind Twitter's biggest decisions.
 Is Vijaya Gadde deftly steering Twitter through murky waters—or permanently alienating a wide swath of Americans, including some in Washington who could ultimately have a say over its future? Gadde herself doesn’t shrink from the notion that she wants to help make a more comfortable and empowering world for marginalized people. She sits on the board of the humanitarian aid group Mercy Corps, and on the side, she  and fellow female Twitter vets run a venture fund to help women rise in the startup world.
 The more Twitter embraces turning from an agnostic platform to one with a particular idea of what makes a “healthy” conversation, the more the question becomes this: Can you really stop a global social network from turning into a cesspool without turning into the speech police?
 “What I’ve been doing for the last nine years is trying to figure out the path,” Gadde said. And that path has led Twitter to a fraught place just before Election Day.
 “I don’t really see an easy solution for how you moderate content at scale around the world,” Gadde said in a July interview. “There’s going to be errors, and there’s going to be corrections, and there’s going to be inconsistencies.”
  The Post story was a perfect example of just how hard Twitter’s new, more restrictive approach can be, especially with fast-moving news.  On October 14, Twitter quickly moved to block users from posting links to a shocking, if unsubstantiated, article on the misdeeds of Hunter Biden, son of the Democratic presidential nominee. The concern, as Twitter would eventually describe it: The materials at the center of the story had been hacked, and revealed the personal information of people featured in the story. It was just 20 days before Election Day, but social media companies were on hair-trigger alert for shenanigans; law enforcement had been warning about threats to the 2020 election,  including “hack and leak” operations.
 Twitter’s ban,  including the locking of an account of a prominent POLITICO reporter who tweeted the story, drew attention away from Facebook which—uncharacteristically—had gotten ahead of Twitter by announcing it was  reducing the distribution of the story while fact checkers looked at it. Said the FCC’s Carr, “The best thing to happen to Facebook was Twitter telling it, ‘Hold my beer.’”
 Individual enforcement decisions are made down the chain at Twitter, but the next days it was on Gadde to come out and explain on her Twitter account, @vijaya, how Twitter was changing course amid the blowback: “After reflecting on this feedback, we have decided to make changes to the policy and how we enforce it,” she wrote, explaining that it was ratcheting back its “hacked materials” policy, though other rules, like its restrictions on posting personal information, still applied.
 Some didn’t buy Gadde’s explanation that the original block was the straightforward application of existing policy.  Tweeted Will Chamberlain,  editor-in-chief of the conservative publication Human Events, “Hi @vijaya ! What evidence do you have that the Hunter Biden materials were hacked? That would seem like a prerequisite to invoking your ‘Hacked Materials Policy.’” And her nuance about what other Twitter policies the Post link violated got lost in the furor. When users found that they still couldn’t post the Post link, many began to wonder if Twitter was talking out of both sides of its mouth.
  Two days after the Post story first ran, and just 18 days before Election Day,  Twitter completely reversed course: Whatever personal details contained in the Post story were already out there, the company said, so users were now free to tweet it.
 Dorsey was among those unhappy with how Twitter handled the situation,  tweeting that the company’s communicating around about the Post situation was “not great,” and that its ham-handed blocking of the story without telling users why was “unacceptable.”
 By then, though, Republicans had already seized on it as part of a pattern, one where Twitter’s edge cases seemed to always run against conservatives. Why could users tweet a link to a New York Times story based on Trump’s leaked financial records, but not a New York Post story on Hunter Biden’s emails? Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Tex.) called it "an obvious and transparent attempt by Twitter to influence the upcoming presidential election.”  The Republican National Committee filed a complaint with the Federal Election Commission. The Senate promised to get to the bottom of it in hearings. And the day after Twitter and Facebook clamped down on the Post story, the chair of the FCC announced his agency would start a formal process to “clarify” how communications law applies to online platforms—widely seen as a threat to the freedom from liability with which, up to now, Twitter and its counterparts have operated.
  Gadde, 45 years old and 5-foot-4, has helped steer Twitter through rapids before. She arrived in 2011 at another pivotal time. The  5-year-old  company was by then an overgrown startup still held together by spit and duct tape, with its unofficial mascot the “fail whale,” the cute cartoon animal that popped up whenever its strained servers went down, again. But as a platform, Twitter was quickly becoming a force in global events—its 50 million users were spread worldwide, and its role in the recent Iranian protests and the Arab Spring revolutions had people musing whether there might be something serious about what had been a faintly goofy chat service for tech insiders.
 Gadde had cut her teeth at the powerhouse Silicon Valley law firm of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, and as Twitter made plans to go public, her job was to avoid the messes that had plagued other high-profile launches. Facebook’s 2012 initial public offering had been “a fiasco,” in the words of the Wall Street Journal, marred by everything from technical glitches to what some investors said was the withholding of key information. Google’s 2004 IPO used an untested model that let investors lowball the search engine’s stock. Twitter didn’t want the same embarrassment. By then, Gadde had been there for two years, working on mergers and acquisitions, and the company’s executives and board had come to view her as a steady hand on the tiller. Said Alex MacGillivray, Twitter’s then-general counsel, who hired Gadde, the top ranks of the company “liked what they saw” in the lawyer.
 Twitter’s IPO was smooth. Its lead banker at Goldman Sachs  tweeted, simply, “Phew!” Gadde, who had been made general counsel just prior, was on her way to developing a reputation as what one-time Twitter head of global public policy Colin Crowell calls “the calm at the eye of every storm.”
 As the company grew up and Twitter stabilized as a business, another element became increasingly important: the ethics and influence of social media. For years, the working assumption was summed up in a 2011 blog post by co-founder Biz Stone: “The tweets must flow.” The policy was to leave tweets alone unless they broke laws or were explicitly spam, thinking the best antidote to bad tweets was letting them be swamped by good tweets.
 In October 2015, Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey,  who’d previously been ousted by the board, returned. Matured by his experience founding the payment firm Square, he embraced the idea of running a mission-driven company that was also a good business. Twitter needed both advertisers and new users, but some of the online world was recoiling at the free-for-all the platform had become.
 When it came to rethinking the free-for-all approach,  Dorsey had a ready ally in Gadde. Six months earlier,  she’d argued in the pages of the Washington Post that Twitter had to stop sacrificing the collective safety of the service on the altar of the individual’s freedom to tweet. “Balancing both aspects of this belief—welcoming diverse perspectives while protecting our users—requires vigilance, and a willingness to make hard choices,” she wrote, and then issued an apology: “That is an ideal that we have at times failed to live up to in recent years.”
 While Facebook is still largely the Mark Zuckerberg show, Dorsey’s style is different. He splits his time running Square, leaving considerable space for other executives to wield day-to-day influence at Twitter. Insiders and close observers of the company say he is willing  to defer to other company leaders, often to Gadde—whose near-decade at Twitter makes her an entrenched veteran at a company that’s seen significant turnover at the executive level. Said Susan Benesch, an academic who as part of the  Dangerous Speech Project she leads has worked closely with Twitter: “It’s hard to even understand how she manages to do so much. She has several difficult and time-consuming jobs.”
 Said Dorsey, via email, “Vijaya brings critical balance to our work. She believes deeply in our purpose, and thinks ahead of all the challenges we’ll be facing in the future. I’m so grateful she works tirelessly to make us better every day, ensuring we’re always considering our role and impact in the world.” (Said the Twitter official who said Dorsey rarely tells Gadde no: “If she wasn’t there, I think people would get away with a lot more stuff.”)
 After the 2016 election, Twitter was condemned by many in Washington for seeming to  let misinformation run wild. Dorsey eventually won praise in Washington for  confronting the complaints head-on, testifying in Congress with Gadde seated just behind him. But Twitter still faced criticisms that the service was a near bottomless pit of vitriol, full of racist attacks, sexist trolling, anti-Semitic pile-ons and dog-piling that could often spiral out of control, while the target complained fruitlessly. Slowly, haltingly, the company began rolling out new policies—like a more robust stance against abuse—and enforcing others, like purging fake accounts from the site.
 That was all still in the air when, in August 2018, controversy flared up over why Twitter hadn’t banned right-wing provocateur Alex Jones, a step Facebook, Apple and YouTube had already taken. The New Yorker’s  daily cartoon that week featured a sketch of Jones and read, “Don’t worry, you’re still welcome on conspiracist-friendly platforms like Twitter and the subway.” That same day,  Dorsey tweeted, “definitely not happy with where our policies are. They need to evolve. Doing that work.”
 Then, something seemed to snap inside Twitter. The platform banned Jones the next month, announced  a new policy on dehumanizing language the following July aimed at addressing the offline harms of such speech, and then in October moved on its political ad ban. It followed up with  a policy on so-called manipulated media, like deep fakes. And in the spring of 2020,  as the Covid-19 pandemic struck, Twitter rolled out a new set of changes meant to thwart the spread of dangerous information about the virus.
  Twitter’s path stands in stark contrast to the one its larger rival Facebook has chosen. Zuckerberg—who has said again and again that Facebook should not be the world’s “arbiter of truth”—has in recent years staked out a position far closer to free-speech-absolutism, detailed in a speech at Georgetown University last year and backed up by  head of policy and communications Nick Clegg, a former British pol who has said of Facebook, “censoring or stifling political discourse would be at odds with what we are about.”
 Inside Facebook, some executives point out that Twitter has a much smaller stream of content to patrol. Twitter has about 4,900 employees; Dorsey won’t say how many of them, or outside contractors, work on content moderation, in part because it’s a shifting number based on world events. Facebook has about 45,000 employees, and the company doesn’t dispute reporting that found the company has 15,000 around the globe working as contractors on content moderation.
 Gadde is quick to say that content moderation is a team job, and also an extremely difficult one. “Speech is one of the most anthropologically complex things imaginable,” wrote Gadde in an email in August, “and our rules have to meet that complexity.”
 In March 2019, that complexity was the centerpiece of a podcast episode hosted by Jo
